---
title: "ngs101_scRNAseq_walkthrough_3 - Part 2. Compare integration methods and select"
author: "Scot J Matkovich; Lei Guo"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    code_folding: hide
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 80
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Description

Primary source:

<https://ngs101.com/how-to-analyze-single-cell-rna-seq-data-complete-beginners-guide-part-3-integration-and-clustering>

## Libraries and directories

```{r step1_libs_data}

# Core single-cell analysis
library(Seurat)
library(SeuratObject)
library(BPCells)
library(glmGamPoi) # speeds up SCTransform
 
# Integration methods
library(harmony)
library(batchelor)
library(SeuratWrappers)
 
# Visualization and data manipulation
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)
 
# Quality metrics and utilities
library(FNN)              # K-nearest neighbor calculations for mixing metrics
library(cluster)          # Silhouette scores for clustering quality
library(reshape2)         # Data reshaping for visualization
 
# Parallel processing
library(future)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 40 * 1024^3)  # Increase to 40GB for large datasets - needed during integration steps
 
# Set working directory
base.dir <- "~/GSE174609_scRNA"
# qc.dir <- file.path(base.dir,"qc_analysis_multi_loop")
# if (!dir.exists(qc.dir)) dir.create(qc.dir, recursive = TRUE)
filt.dir <- file.path(base.dir,"filtered_data")
if (!dir.exists(filt.dir)) dir.create(filt.dir, recursive = TRUE)
integr.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "integration_analysis")

# Create output directories
dir.create(file.path(integr.dir,"plots"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(integr.dir,"plots/integration_comparison"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(integr.dir,"plots/clustering"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(integr.dir,"integrated_data"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(integr.dir,"metadata"), showWarnings = FALSE)

# Object file names from Part 1 integration analysis
merged_naive.file <- "merged_naive.rds"
merged_integrations.file <- "merged_seurat_with_integrations.rds"

# Set random seed for reproducibility
set.seed(27)

# Configure plotting defaults
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 12))
```

## Load integration comparison objects from Part 1

```{r step2_load_objs}

cat("Reading in merged_naive Seurat object:",merged_naive.file,"\n")
merged_naive <- readRDS(file.path(integr.dir,"integrated_data",merged_naive.file))
cat("Reading in merged Seurat object with integrations:",merged_integrations.file,"\n")
merged_seurat <- readRDS(file.path(integr.dir,"integrated_data",merged_integrations.file))

```

## Visual comparison of UMAPs

```{r umap_comparison}
#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 11: Generate comparison UMAPs
#-----------------------------------------------
 
cat("\n=== Generating Comparison Plots ===\n")
 
# Create a function to make standardized UMAP plots
make_comparison_plot <- function(seurat_obj, reduction_name, title, group_by = "sample_id", colors = NULL) {
  p <- DimPlot(seurat_obj, reduction = reduction_name, group.by = group_by, pt.size = 0.05) +
    ggtitle(title) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
          legend.key.size = unit(0.3, "cm"))
 
  # Apply custom colors if provided
  if (!is.null(colors)) {
    p <- p + scale_color_manual(values = colors)
  }
 
  return(p)
}
 
# UMAPs colored by sample (assesses mixing)
p_sample_naive <- make_comparison_plot(merged_naive, "umap", "Naive Merge", 
                                       colors = sample_colors)
p_sample_cca <- make_comparison_plot(merged_seurat, "umap.cca", "CCA", 
                                     colors = sample_colors)
p_sample_rpca <- make_comparison_plot(merged_seurat, "umap.rpca", "RPCA", 
                                      colors = sample_colors)
p_sample_harmony <- make_comparison_plot(merged_seurat, "umap.harmony", "Harmony", 
                                         colors = sample_colors)
p_sample_mnn <- make_comparison_plot(merged_seurat, "umap.mnn", "FastMNN", 
                                     colors = sample_colors)
 
# Combine sample-colored UMAPs
combined_samples <- (p_sample_naive | p_sample_cca | p_sample_rpca) / 
                    (p_sample_harmony | p_sample_mnn | plot_spacer())
 
ggsave(file.path(integr.dir,"plots/integration_comparison/02_integration_by_sample.png"), 
       combined_samples, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300)
 
# UMAPs colored by condition (assesses biological preservation)
p_cond_naive <- make_comparison_plot(merged_naive, "umap", "Naive Merge", "condition", 
                                     colors = condition_colors)
p_cond_cca <- make_comparison_plot(integrated_cca, "umap.cca", "CCA", "condition", 
                                   colors = condition_colors)
p_cond_rpca <- make_comparison_plot(integrated_rpca, "umap.rpca", "RPCA", "condition", 
                                    colors = condition_colors)
p_cond_harmony <- make_comparison_plot(integrated_harmony, "umap.harmony", "Harmony", "condition", 
                                       colors = condition_colors)
p_cond_mnn <- make_comparison_plot(integrated_fastmnn, "umap.mnn", "FastMNN", "condition", 
                                   colors = condition_colors)
 
# Combine condition-colored UMAPs
combined_conditions <- (p_cond_naive | p_cond_cca | p_cond_rpca) /
                       (p_cond_harmony | p_cond_mnn | plot_spacer())
 
ggsave(file.path(integr.dir,"plots/integration_comparison/03_integration_by_condition.png"),
       combined_conditions, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300)
```
## R sessionInfo

```{r export_sessionInfo}
session.dir <- file.path(base.dir,"sessionInfo")
if (!dir.exists(session.dir)){
  dir.create(session.dir, recursive = T)
}

write.csv(unlist(sessionInfo()), file=file.path(session.dir,"tut3_integration_part2_sessionInfo.csv"))
sessionInfo() |>
  capture.output(file=file.path(session.dir,"tut3_integration_part2_sessionInfo_summary.txt"))

sessionInfo()
```
